title: Structural Freedom

content.question: What structural change would give you the freedom you're chasing via effort?
sop: List everything that currently requires your direct, personal involvement to function.
Rate your Automation, Alignment, and Ownership each 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), automation of tasks, alignment of structure with your actual goals, ownership of the assets involved.
Pick the lowest-scoring variable and design one structural fix (a system, a hire, a contract change) rather than just working harder.
Defend that structural boundary the next time a shortcut tempts you to skip it.
Re-score quarterly, freedom decays if the structure isn't maintained.
questions: What structural change would give you the freedom you're chasing via effort?
If you took a month completely off, what would break, and why does it still depend on you?
Are you designing your business's structure on purpose, or did it just accumulate by default?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Automation, Alignment, Ownership. Automation 6 times Alignment 8 times Ownership 7 equals 336. Higher means your structure produces freedom without your constant input; lower means your day-to-day still depends on you personally showing up.
content.closing_statement: Design the structure instead of just working harder.

caption:
Principle Nº 179: Structural Freedom

Freedom is designed, not earned.

Ask yourself: What structural change would give you the freedom you're chasing via effort?
